body.title: [sprint] proof_emitter: production-only revert mode for test+impl-in-one-commit
body.intention: I intend that proof_emitter can isolate an authentic red when the test assertion and the implementation are committed together — by inverting only the non-test (production) portion of parent..HEAD, not the whole diff.
body.description:
proof_emitter generates red by inverting the parent..HEAD diff in place (proof_emitter.py ~line 40-42). When a test's new assertion and the production change that satisfies it land in the SAME commit (the common case — code + its test together), inverting the whole commit reverts both, so old-test + old-code agree and the red comes back GREEN ('could not generate authentic red — test passes in pre-implementation state'). This blocks proven-close for an entire class of legitimate single-commit changes (e.g. T-skills-goals-to-intentions: removing links.goals from the schema + updating the schema-assertion test — manual red→green confirmed real: goals in _LINK_KEYS makes test_envelope_schema_matches_memory_emit fail 'Extra items: goals', removed it passes).

**Proposed shape:** add a revert mode that inverts only the PRODUCTION (non-test) files in parent..HEAD, leaving the current (new) test on disk during the red window — yielding new-test + old-production = authentic red. Detect test files by path (tests/ or test_*.py) or by an explicit --production-paths arg. Keep the existing whole-diff invert as default; the new mode is opt-in for bundled test+impl commits.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/proof_emitter.py (_git_inplace_red and prove()); tests/test_proof_emitter.py (add a bundled-commit case).
**Design rules:** proof-on-close (this widens the set of provable closes — earns gate removal), prove-what's-load-bearing.
**Scope boundary:** IN - production-only revert mode + its test. OUT - multi-commit thing-spanning red (separate known limitation, proof_emitter.py line 64-67).
**Test plan:** a test where assertion+impl are one commit; assert prove() generates authentic red via production-only revert and emits a HEAD-bound proof.
**Completion criteria:** proof_emitter, run on a single commit bundling a test assertion change with the production change that satisfies it, emits a valid red->green proof (no 'could not generate authentic red').
